title: Alpha Engine attractiveness_eval artifact (eval-storage contract, v2)
description: FROZEN cross-repo contract for backtest/{date}/attractiveness_eval.json — produced by crucible-backtester analysis/attractiveness_eval.py, consumed by crucible-evaluator. Measures the universe-board attractiveness composite against realized forward market-relative log-alpha (config#1389 per-pillar IC + suggested weights, config#1392 trajectory forward-IC, config#1398 top-N counterfactual vs live tech_score gate). All alpha values are DECIMAL log-alpha (0.043 = 4.3%), never percent. Missing/immature sub-blocks carry honest null values and n-fields — never fabricated zeros. Additive-only evolution: consumers MUST tolerate unknown extra fields; renames/removals require a schema_version bump coordinated with the consumer. LIFTED from crucible-backtester contracts/attractiveness_eval.schema.json into nousergon_lib.contracts on the second-adoption signal (config#1861). V2 CHANGE (config#1861, horizon-is-a-parameter doctrine — ARCHITECTURE §48 / config#1483): counterfactual mean_alpha_21d is renamed to mean_alpha in both top_n[] and live_gate — the horizon is given by the top-level horizon_days parameter and must not be hardcoded into the field name. Consumers keep a v1 (mean_alpha_21d) read-tolerance window for 1 week after the coordinated cutover per S3 contract safety.

schema_version.description: Contract version stamp — matches nousergon_lib.contracts.SCHEMA_VERSIONS['attractiveness_eval']. Bumped 1→2 for the mean_alpha_21d→mean_alpha rename (config#1861).

horizon_days.description: Forward-return horizon in trading days, from nousergon_lib.quant.horizons.DEFAULT_POLICY.primary_horizon (21 under the canonical-alpha framework) — a parameter, never hardcoded by the producer. As of v2 the counterfactual alpha field name (mean_alpha) no longer duplicates this horizon.

reason.description: Present when status != ok (or on degraded sub-blocks): the honest, human-readable insufficiency.

pillar_ic.description: Per-pillar date-clustered ICs (config#1389). Keys are the pillar column names of the attractiveness history parquet (schema authority: crucible-research scoring/attractiveness_history.py — currently quality/value/momentum/growth/stewardship/defensiveness; keys are dynamic so a research-side pillar change flows through).

suggested_pillar_weights.description: SUGGESTED weights only — advisory content inside this eval artifact. The producer NEVER writes config/factor_attractiveness_weights.json or any live config. IC-proportional weights shrunk toward 1/N (DeMiguel/Garlappi/Uppal); sum to 1.0 when non-empty.

lambda.description: Weight on the 1/N prior. 1.0 at n_eval_dates <= 8 (the suggested weights ARE 1/N), decaying as 8/n above.

trajectory_ic.description: Forward-IC of the persisted attractiveness-trajectory signals (config#1392), from scanner/universe/trajectory/{date}/trajectory.json joined to realized forward alpha.

counterfactual.description: Top-N-by-attractiveness selections vs the live tech_score survivor gate (config#1398). capture_rate = per-cycle fraction of the cycle's ex-post top-K realized-alpha names (K = selection size, winners from the full scanner-evaluated cycle universe) present in the selection, averaged equally across cycles. mean_alpha = per-cycle selection mean realized forward log-alpha over horizon_days, averaged equally across cycles (date-clustered, decimal). Renamed from mean_alpha_21d in v2 (config#1861): the horizon is carried by the top-level horizon_days, not the field name.

ic_block.description: Date-clustered Spearman rank-IC (each weekly cross-section is ONE observation, Grinold-Kahn t-stat; honest small-N — t/p null below 3 weekly observations) + pooled IC/p as the secondary, pseudo-replication-inflated reference.
